_comment: Starter knee-rehab corridor — authored in-house (original instruction text; exercise mechanics are facts, phrasing is ours). Dan + his PT review, correct, and expand this toward the PRD's ~30-40 exercise corridor. No open dataset carries these (verified 2026-07-16).

exercises.name: Quad Set
instructions: Sit or lie back with your surgical leg straight and the other knee comfortably bent.
Optionally place a small rolled towel under the knee of the straight leg.
Tighten the muscle on top of your thigh, pressing the back of the knee down toward the floor. The kneecap slides slightly upward when you do this correctly.
Hold the squeeze, then fully relax before the next repetition.

exercises.name: Ankle Pumps
instructions: Lie back with both legs straight and relaxed.
Slowly point your toes away from you as far as comfortable, then pull them back up toward your shin.
Keep the motion smooth and rhythmic; the goal is circulation, not effort.

exercises.name: Heel Slide
instructions: Lie on your back with both legs straight.
Slide the heel of your surgical leg along the surface toward your buttock, letting the knee bend as far as comfortable.
Optionally loop a towel or strap around your foot to assist the slide.
Pause at the end of the comfortable range, then slide back to straight.

exercises.name: Seated Knee Flexion
instructions: Sit on a firm chair with both feet flat on the floor.
Slide the foot of your surgical leg back under the chair as far as comfortable, letting the knee bend.
For a gentle overpressure, cross your other ankle in front and press lightly backward.
Hold at the end range, then slide the foot forward to relax.

exercises.name: Straight-Leg Raise
instructions: Lie on your back with your surgical leg straight and the other knee bent, foot flat.
Tighten the thigh of the straight leg first (a quad set), locking the knee.
Keeping the knee locked, lift the whole leg until it is level with the opposite thigh.
Lower slowly with control, keeping the knee straight the entire time.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on your back with both knees bent and feet flat, hip-width apart.
Squeeze your buttocks and lift your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Avoid arching the lower back; the lift comes from the hips.
Lower slowly to the floor and relax between repetitions.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Bridge
instructions: Set up as for a glute bridge, then extend one leg so only the working foot stays on the floor.
Squeeze the buttock of the working side and lift the hips, keeping the pelvis level.
If the pelvis drops or twists, regress to the two-leg bridge.
Lower with control and repeat before switching sides.

exercises.name: Clamshell
instructions: Lie on your side with hips and knees bent about 45 degrees, legs stacked.
Keeping your feet together and pelvis still, lift the top knee like a clamshell opening.
Do not let your pelvis roll backward — the motion is small and comes from the hip.
Lower slowly. Add a band just above the knees to progress.

exercises.name: Terminal Knee Extension (TKE)
instructions: Anchor a resistance band at knee height and loop it behind the knee of your working leg, facing the anchor.
Step back until the band is taut, with the working knee slightly bent.
Straighten the knee fully against the band, squeezing the thigh at the top.
Let the band bend the knee slightly, then extend again with control.

exercises.name: Heel Raise
instructions: Stand tall with fingertips on a wall or chair back for balance.
Rise up onto the balls of both feet as high as comfortable.
Pause at the top, then lower slowly with control.
Progress by shifting more weight to the surgical side.

exercises.name: Mini Squat
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a counter or chair back if needed.
Bend both knees to lower a few inches — roughly a quarter of a full squat — keeping knees tracking over toes.
Keep your heels down and chest up.
Push through both feet to stand tall again.

exercises.name: Wall Sit
instructions: Stand with your back flat against a wall, feet about a foot and a half from it, hip-width apart.
Slide down the wall until your knees are bent — start shallow (about 45 degrees) and deepen only as tolerated, never past thighs-parallel.
Keep knees over ankles, not past the toes, and your back pressed into the wall.
Hold, then slide back up. Stop if you feel kneecap pain.

exercises.name: Step-Up
instructions: Stand facing a low step, hands free or one hand on a rail.
Place the foot of your working leg fully on the step.
Push through that heel to lift your body up, bringing the other foot to the step.
Step back down slowly with control, leading with the non-working leg.

exercises.name: Step-Down
instructions: Stand on a low step on your working leg, the other foot hovering off the front edge.
Bend the working knee to slowly lower the free heel toward the floor — the knee stays tracking over the toes, pelvis level.
Touch the heel down lightly without transferring weight, then push back up.
This is an eccentric control drill: the way down is the exercise.

progressions.note: Add hip-flexor load once the quad fires reliably with no extension lag.
Move the isometric squeeze into banded end-range work.
Progress from assisted to active flexion range as swelling settles.
Progress when the two-leg bridge is easy and the pelvis stays level.
Move to closed-chain loading once end-range extension is strong.
Add isometric endurance at a controlled depth.
Eccentric single-leg control — the hardest of the chain; watch knee tracking.
